prologue: ^My village died when I was young.
^I spent twenty years becoming a doctor.
^But the last ingredient — the Moonleaf, the Silverroot —
^they've been extinct for two hundred years.
^The village witch found me. She said she could send me back.
time_transition: ^Five days. That is all she could give me.
^The village with the Moonleaf seems closer.
day1_arrival: ^You are not from here. What do you want?
^My name is Aiden. I am looking for the Moonleaf.
^A cure. For a disease that has taken many lives.
^The Moonleaf only blooms at dawn. Only for those who slept safely nearby through the night.
^Then I need to make camp. Can you help me?
^I can. But first — do you know how to choose a campsite?
^Then come. Two travelers camped here before you. Go and look at what they left behind. The forest will teach you better than I can.
site_a: ^This traveler left before sunrise. Look around carefully. What do you notice?
^Look at the stream beside the tent

c-0: ^The branches hang directly over the tent. Dead wood falls without warning — and they drip long after rain stops.
c-1: ^The stream is barely two steps away. See the waterline on those rocks? The water rises at night.
c-2: ^The ground dips down toward the water here. After any rain this whole area floods.
c-3: ^Nowhere safe to build a fire. Too many roots, too much damp ground, branches too low overhead.

site_b: ^Now come. There is one more site to look at. This traveler left safe and healthy. See what they chose.

c-0: ^The ground sits higher than the area around it. Water drains away naturally — this spot stays dry even after heavy rain.
c-1: ^The boulder blocks the wind and gives a clear view of the surrounding forest. No branches overhead — nothing to fall or drip on the tent.
c-2: ^The stream is about fifteen metres away. The bank is high and stable. Close enough to collect water — far enough to sleep safely.
c-3: ^Open sky directly above. The fire ring sits about three metres from the tent on the downhill side — smoke drifts away from the sleeping area.

challenge1_setup: ^You have seen both sites. Now you need to find your own.
^A new site. A tent pitched on a slope. A fire ring with a branch clearly visible overhead.

c-0: ^Look more carefully. Something here would cost you the night.

challenge1_mcq2: ^Which of these are problems at this site?

c-1: ^Look at the site again. You missed something.
c-2: ^Look at the site again. You missed something.
c-3: ^Are you sure about that one? Look carefully.
c-4: ^Are you sure about that one? Look carefully.

challenge1_mcq3: ^What is the problem with the ground?
^It is too rocky and uneven to sleep on
^It is too close to the tree roots

c-0: ^Rocky is uncomfortable but not dangerous. Think about where the water would go if it rained on that slope.
c-1: ^Exactly. Water runs downhill. A sloped site floods from above.
c-2: ^Roots can be managed. Think about where the water would go if it rained on that slope.
c-3: ^Soft grass is not the danger. Think about where the water would go if it rained on that slope.

challenge1_mcq4: ^What is the problem with the fire?
^The fire is too far from the tent to provide warmth
^The fire ring has a low dry branch directly overhead
^The fire is too close to the stream

c-0: ^Distance is not the issue here. Look directly above the fire ring — that is where the danger is.
c-1: ^Exactly. A single spark and the whole branch goes up.
c-2: ^The stream is not directly above the fire. Look up — that is where the danger is.
c-3: ^Size does not matter here. Look up — that is where the danger is.

challenge2_setup: ^Now you know what to avoid. Let's set up properly before the sun goes down.
^On the raised firm ground behind the boulder
^Under the large tree for overhead cover
^On the flat ground at the base of the hill

c-1: ^Overhanging branches drip and drop wood. This tree carries the same risk.
c-2: ^Flat looks safe but the hill above sends rainwater runoff straight down here.

challenge2_mcq2: ^In the open clearing, 3m from the tent, on the downhill side
^Beside the tent on the uphill side
^Under the tree canopy, sheltered from wind
^At the stream's edge, safer if it spreads

c-0: ^Good. Smoke drifts away. Nothing overhead to catch a spark.
c-1: ^Uphill means smoke and embers drift down toward the tent as you sleep.
c-2: ^Overhanging branches and fire are a bad combination.
c-3: ^Damp ground near water makes fire hard to keep alive and risks spreading to roots and reeds.

challenge2_mcq3: ^Where do you position your shelter opening?
^Toward the large tree for morning shade
^Direction does not matter for one night

c-0: ^Correct. Cold air and smoke stay out.
c-1: ^Facing the stream means damp air and insects flow straight into the tent all night.
c-2: ^Morning shade is comfort, not safety. Wind direction determines where cold air and smoke go while you sleep.
c-3: ^One night of bad wind direction means a cold, smoky sleep.

challenge2_complete: ^The camp is set. Rest now. The Moonleaf will find you at dawn.
day1_dawn: ^Thank you, Mara. For everything. I have to get going now — to save everyone.
day2_transition: ^One herb down. One more to go.
day2_arrival: ^Rain is coming. I need to make camp now, before it hits.
^Everything Mara taught me. Let's see if I remember it.
day2_campsite_mcq: ^Flat ground under thick tree cover. Stream nearby. Protected from wind.
^Raised firm ground behind a boulder. Stream 15m away. Open sky above.
^Hollow between two rocks. Very sheltered from wind. Dry overhead canopy.

c-0: ^Tree cover drips long after rain stops and risks falling branches. It does nothing to stop flooding. In rain, what the ground does with water matters more than what is above you.
c-1: ^Raised ground. Distance from water. Everything else is secondary when it rains.
c-2: ^Hollows collect rainwater and flood overnight. In rain, elevation always beats shelter.

day2_campsite_correct: ^Now I'll need to set things up before it rains.
day2_fire_intro: ^You are staring at a pile of wood like it owes you something.
^I want to build a fire to keep warm. But I do not know where to start.
^Then I'll show you. But we need to move fast — that rain won't wait.
day2_fire_spot: ^I know this. I have done it before.
^Under a large tree, sheltered from rain
^In the open, 3m from tent, downhill, clear sky above
^Right beside the tent on the uphill side
^At the edge of the clearing near the stream

c-0: ^Tree branches overhead drip constantly in rain and dry ones catch sparks easily.
c-2: ^Uphill means smoke and embers drift down toward you all night.
c-3: ^Damp ground near water makes fire impossible to maintain.

day2_fire_clear: ^Now clear the ground before you do anything else. Leaves, roots, debris — fire spreads to whatever is closest.
day2_fire_wood: ^Not all wood burns the same. Dry, dead wood — that is what you want.
day2_end: ^There. That will hold through the night.
